transcript: I made John's lamb stew today. I have not cooked it since before he died, nearly two years now. I was not sure I would remember the recipe but my hands just seemed to know what to do. The whole kitchen smelled exactly like it used to on a Sunday. I sat and ate a bowl of it on my own and felt his absence very strongly. It was painful but also strangely comforting. I think I will make it again. It felt like keeping something alive.
